LAW AND MOTION 302, BAY AREA SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. CROSS-DEFENDANT BAY AREA SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS, INC.'S ("BASS") REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR EXPRESS INDEMNITY, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT (FACC) PLEADS THAT THE OWNER OF THE PROPERTY HAS ASSESSED LIQUIDATED DAMAGES AGAINST CROSS-COMPLAINANT RANGER PIPELINES, INC. ("RANGER"), (FACC, PAR. 16.) THE FACC ALSO PLEADS THAT THE SUBCONTRACT PROVIDES THAT CROSS-DEFENDANT BASS MUST INDEMNIFY CROSS-COMPLAINANT RANGER "FROM AND AGAINST ALL ACTIONS, PENALTIES, ASSESSMENTS'DEMANDS, LIABILITIES, CLAIMS, DAMAGES, COSTS, LOSSES, AND EXPENSES." (FACC, PAR. 9, EX. A.) A GENERAL DEMURRER DOES NOT LIE TO ONLY PART OF A CAUSE OF ACTION AND THEREFORE THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED TO THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IN ITS ENTIRETY. (SEE KONG V. CITY OF HAWAIIN GARDENS REDEVELOP. AGENCY (2003) 108 CAL.APP.4TH 1028, 1047.) AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. (CCP SEC. 430.10(E).) THE "ACTUAL CONTROVERSIES" ALLEGED IN THE FACC HAVE ALREADY "RIPENED INTO A VIOLATION OF A CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATION." (RIMINGTON V. GENERAL ACE. GROUP OF INS. COS. (1962) 205 CAL.APP.2D 394, 397.) THE UNDERLYING SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF MR. WIRE COMMUNICATIONS IS BASED ON ALLEGATIONS THAT CROSS-COMPLAINANT RANGER PROVIDED DEFECTIVE DESIGNS AND THE RESULTANT DAMAGES HAVE ALREADY OCCURRED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. GRANTED, PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 426.50. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ("REGENTS") FAILURE TO FILE A CROSS COMPLAINT WAS IN GOOD FAITH. NO TRIAL DATE IS YET SET. Bay Area Systems & Solutions, Inc.'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. BAY AREA SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS, INC.'s Demurrer To 1st Amended Cross-Complaint is Overruled in part and sustained in part. Cross-Defendant Bay Area Systems & Solutions, Inc.'s ("Bass") request for judicial notice is granted. As to the first cause of action for express indemnity, the demurrer is overruled. The First Amended Cross-Complaint (FACC) pleads that the owner of the property has assessed liquidated damages against Cross-Complainant Ranger Pipelines, Inc. ("Ranger"), (FACC, par. 16.) The FACC also pleads that the subcontract provides that Cross-Defendant Bass must indemnify Cross-Complainant Ranger "from and against all actions, penalties, assessments'demands, liabilities, claims, damages, costs, losses, and expenses." (FACC, par. 9, Ex. A.) A general demurrer does not lie to only part of a cause of action and therefore the demurrer is overruled to this cause of action in its entirety. (See Kong v. City of Hawaiin Gardens Redevelop. Agency (2003) 108 Cal.App.4th 1028, 1047.) As to the second cause of action for declaratory relief, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) The "actual controversies" alleged in the FACC have already "ripened into a violation of a contractual obligation." (Rimington v. General Ace. Group of Ins. Cos. (1962) 205 Cal.App.2d 394, 397.) The underlying Second Amended Complaint filed by Plaintiff Mr. Wire Communications is based on allegations that Cross-Complainant Ranger provided defective designs and the resultant damages have already occurred. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

The Regents Of The University Of California'S Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint is Granted, pursuant to CCP section 426.50. Defendant The Regents of the University of California ("Regents") failure to file a cross complaint was in good faith. No trial date is yet set. A motion for leave to file a compulsory cross-complaint "shall be liberally construed to avoid forfeiture of causes of action." CCP sec. 426.50. Plaintiff Mr. Wire has not established that Regents cannot state a valid cause of action in the proposed cross-complaint. Regents may file its proposed cross complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. The Court modified and signed the order that was submitted by email. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: roy bartlett, esq., 925.324.5150, and margaret may, esq., 415.599.6159, for plaintiff mr wire communications.
stephanie rocha, esq., 408.292.1765, for defendant regents of the university of california.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, the demurrer to the third cause of action for misrepresentation, overruled as to the second cause of action, and sustained, without leave to amend, as to the fourth cause of action. For the second cause of action, the Economic Loss Rule does not preclude Plaintiff's claims. For the third cause of action, as pled, Plaintiff's misrepresentation claims are barred by Government Code 918.8. For the fourth cause of action, unjust enrichment is not a cause of action. Leave to amend is granted, if Plaintiff can allege in good faith, its status as a California licensed contractor and to allege a cause of action for a breach of the implied warranty of correctness. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
